title: carpinus rankanensis
intro: Carpinus rankanensis, also known as Japanese hop hornbeam, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive horn-like buds and heart-shaped leaves. The bark and leaves of Carpinus rankanensis have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carpinus rankanensis include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and sore throats, and acting as a diuretic. In traditional Chinese medicine, Carpinus rankanensis is often prepared as a tea or decoction by boiling the bark or leaves in water to harness its medicinal properties.
